## A note on sensitive data

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Common cases:**

- Endangered or commonly poached species locations
- Species/locations with economic sensitivity

**Options → you don't have to choose between "share *everything*" or "share *nothing*":**

- **Generalize location** - provide a region or polygon (`footprintWKT`) instead of an exact point
- **Delay publication** - prepare to publish but set an embargo for later
- **Generalize taxonomic name** - provide a higher taxon name
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](assets/polygon-wkt.png)
:::
::::

*Note: Generalizations should be documented alongside your data!*
*See [OBIS Manual](https://manual.obis.org/contribute.html#how-to-handle-sensitive-data) & [GBIF Guide](https://docs.gbif.org/sensitive-species-best-practices/master/en/) for more details*

::: {.notes}
Sensitivity is not a reason to skip publishing. There's a spectrum of options short of full open release. GBIF also has a good best-practices doc on generalizing sensitive species data if anyone wants to go deeper (Chapman 2020, GBIF Secretariat).
There are tools for generalizing locations (OBIS Map Tool, Marine Regions Gazetteer) in the OBIS manual + GBIF guide
:::
